data.title: Regeneron drops after court rejects request for temporary injunction for Eylea generic
data.description:
• Regeneron Pharmaceutical (NASDAQ:REGN) fell 2.4% after a court rejected its request for a temporary injunction blocking sales of its generic Eylea.
  • Marketed globally by Regeneron (REGN) and its German partner Bayer (OTCPK:BAYZF), Eylea has generated $5.7B in sales for the company in the U.S. in 2023.
  • Developing story ...

data.title: Nucor sinks after Q3 profit drops, seeing further weakness in Q4
data.description:
Nucor (NYSE:NUE) -7.4% in Tuesday's trading, on track for its biggest one-day percentage drop in six months, after reporting better than expected Q3 adjusted earnings that nevertheless plunged from a year ago, and the steelmaker said it expects another drop in profit for Q4, with sales prices and volumes likely to fall further.

Q3 net income fell to $249.9M, or $1.05/share, from $1.14B, or $4.57/share, in the year-earlier quarter, and revenues fell 15% Y/Y to $7.44B but edged analyst expectations.

Nucor (NUE) said its Q3 average sales price per ton fell 6% Q/Q and 15% Y/Y, as it shipped 6.19M tons to outside customers during the quarter, down 1% Q/Q and Y/Y.

"Overall, in the near-term Nucor continues to face a challenging environment," as the company indicated it expects earnings to drop below $1.05/share, according to BMO analyst Katja Jancic, who rates the stock at Market Perform.

Other steel producers also trade lower, including Cleveland-Cliffs (CLF) -3%, Steel Dynamics (STLD) -1.8% and US Steel (X) -0.7%.

data.title: Estée Lauder merchandise available in the Amazon Premium Beauty Store
data.description:
Estée Lauder (NYSE:EL) makeup and skincare is now available in the Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) Premium Beauty store, providing Amazon (AMZN) Prime members with free delivery on their favorite products. Estée Lauder’s (EL) Clinique brand has been available at the Amazon Beauty Store since March.

“The launch of Estée Lauder in Amazon Premium Beauty marks a huge milestone for our brand as we bring our high-performance products and advanced skincare science to Amazon customers in the U.S.,” said Justin Boxford, Global Brand President for Estée Lauder.

The cosmetics and fragrance company is also debuting a Virtual Foundation Tool that is exclusive to Amazon (AMZN). Shoppers can compare multiple shares of foundation virtually and add their choice into an Amazon shopping cart.

To amplify its brand presence on Amazon (AMZN), the company is launching a female-centered sports marketing campaign called Game Day, Your Way, featuring Double Wear Stay-In-Place makeup. The ad will air throughout fall 2024.

The news has failed to give a lift to shares, with the sector weighed down by a warning from French cosmetics giant L'Oréal (OTCPK:LRLCY) regarding the increasingly challenging Chinese ecosystem. Accordingly, shares of Estée Lauder (EL), Coty (COTY), Ulta (ULTA), and e.l.f. Beauty (ELF) are all lower by 2% to as much as 5%.

data.title: Salesforce current valuation is compelling, activist Starboard Value says
data.description:
Activist investor Starboard Value, which first began pushing for changes at Salesforce (NYSE:CRM) about two years ago, said the company's current valuation is compelling.

"Salesforce’s current valuation is compelling, especially if the company can reach, and exceed, the Rule of 50," Starboard said in a presentation given at the 13D Monitor Active-Passive Investor Summit in New York City on Thursday.

Starboard believes that Salesforce (CRM) can reach the Rule of 50 and generate more than $20 of free cash flow per share in FY2028.

Salesforce has driven "meaningful improvements" in sales and marketing and G&A efficiency since FY2023, and the Starboard believes it can continue to drive "incremental efficiencies" in the categories, according to the presentation.

It was first disclosed in October 2022 that Starboard had taken a "significant" stake in Salesforce (CRM) and that the activist wanted the company to work on improving its margins.

Shares of Salesforce (CRM) ticked down by 1% on Tuesday.

data.title: Retail jolt: Cotti Coffee opened its 10,000th store as the growth story continues
data.description:
Cotti Coffee announced that it opened its 10,000th store, with an opening in Doha, Qatar. The milestone arrived only two years after the anniversary of the company's first shop.

The company emerged in 2022 from the ashes of Luckin Coffee's (OTCPK:LKNCY) financial scandal. The brand quickly made its mark in China's competitive coffee market, opening its first store in Fuzhou on October 22, 2022. Cotti's rapid expansion strategy mirrored Luckin's (OTCPK:LKNCY), leveraging aggressive marketing, low prices, and a franchise model to fuel growth. Within just five months, Cotti reached 1,000 stores nationwide. The Cotti brand gained visibility by sponsoring the Argentina national football team during the 2022 World Cup and launching limited-edition products. By December 2023, Cotti had established over 6,000 coffee shops, and expanded internationally with stores in South Korea, Indonesia, and plans for other Asian markets.

Cotti Coffee's major competitors in China include Luckin Coffee (OTCPK:LKNCY), Starbucks (NASDAQ:SBUX), Costa Coffee (KO), Gryebox Coffee, McDonald's (MCD) McCafe, Yum China's (YUMC) KCCcoffee, Pacific Coffee, Manner Coffee, and Seesaw Coffee.

data.title: Larger deficit under Trump; larger corporate headwinds under Harris – pick your fighter
data.description:
Both Harris and Trump could be affecting the U.S. economy in different ways: one by adding a lot more to the national deficit, the other by creating headwinds to corporations in the form of bigger taxes.

Governmental spending deficit expansion in 2026 will happen regardless of who wins the 2024 election, according to Morgan Stanley U.S. public policy strategists and Valuation, Accounting and Tax strategists. But a bigger deficit is skewed towards a Republican “sweep” outcome.

On the other hand, the potential for corporate tax rate increases could be at play in a Democratic “sweep,” analysts said.

According to next-twelve-month earnings estimates, if the corporate tax rate increases to 23%, this would result in earnings headwinds for the S&P 500 (SP500) of about 1.5%, said Katy L. Huberty, the global head of research at Morgan Stanley, in a note.

In addition, a 25% rate would translate to about a 3.5% headwind, and a 28% rate would create a 5.5% earnings headwind.

Consumer staples (XLP), consumer discretionary (XLY), communication services (XLC), industrials (XLI), financials (XLF), and energy (XLP) have the highest EPS exposure to a rate increase, Huberty said.

Utilities (XLU) are also exposed; however, rate-regulated utilities could receive relief in their rate base, she said.

Lastly, according to Morgan Stanley’s U.S. equity strategist, “markets are rotating more toward a “Trump win” scenario (resembling 2016 election playbook) – although they note that polls are much closer to neutral than that equity market signal.”

“After Trump's win in 2016, small-business confidence saw its largest three-month increase in its history (40+ years), while individual investor sentiment also spiked,” Huberty added, and explained that analysts are calling this “animal spirits.”

“Our team thinks markets may be trying to front-run a repeat of the 2016 outcome, adding that if equities are indeed pricing in a Trump win.”

Meaning, if Harris wins, the markets could be facing the risk of some reversion in their recent performance, Huberty concluded.

data.title: U.S. prepares new sanctions to curb Russia's military capabilities, Yellen says
data.description:
U.S. Treasury Secretary Janet Yellen said Tuesday the U.S. is preparing a new wave of sanctions targeting Russia's access to goods critical for its military.

The measures, which would be announced as soon as next week, would be the latest in a string of restrictions aimed at weakening Russia's ability to sustain its war efforts by tightening controls on vital resources.

The Biden administration will "unveil strong new sanctions targeting those facilitating the Kremlin’s war machine, including intermediaries in third countries that are supplying Russia with critical inputs for its military,” Yellen said in Washington, D.C.

Alongside focusing on intermediaries in countries of concern, the U.S. is anticipated to introduce additional measures aimed at Russia's energy sector and depository institutions, Bloomberg reported, citing people familiar with the matter.

Russia has been circumventing sanctions by buying dual-use items directly from third countries to support its military machine in the invasion of Ukraine, the article said, noting that Ukraine has been pressing its allies to intensify efforts to block Russia's sanction evasion and reduce its oil reserves.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Contineum draws bullish view at Baird on de-risked assets
data.description:
Following a three-day selloff, Contineum Therapeutics (NASDAQ:CTNM) traded higher on Tuesday after Baird launched its coverage with an Outperform recommendation, noting that data for other treatments have already de-risked its two clinical-stage assets.

Analyst Joel Beatty pointed to Contineum's (NASDAQ:CTNM) lead assets PIPE-791 and PIPE-307, noting that while the duo is still in the early stages of development, "three of the four indications they're in development for are partially de-risked by clinical data from other agents."

Beatty argued that despite having a similar mechanism to Bristol Myers Squibb's (BMY) Phase 3 agent BMS-986278, PIPE-791, which targets the lung disorder idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis, has a better dosing regimen and efficacy compared to the rival's candidate.

The analyst also noted that Contineum's (CTNM) lead asset PIPE-307, a collaboration with Johnson & Johnson (JNJ), is partially de-risked by third-party clinical data for FDA-approved drugs, clemastine and scopolamine.

Beatty issued a $32 price target on CTNM, arguing that the end of a mid-stage trial for PIPE-307 in relapsing-remitting multiple sclerosis (RMMS) could be a potential catalyst for the stock in 2025.

data.title: Zeta initiated with Overweight rating on consistent customer growth: KeyBanc
data.description:
Zeta Global Holdings (NYSE:ZETA) received an Overweight rating by KeyBanc as the investment firm initiated coverage on the marketing technology company.

"Through its data assets and customer data platform, Zeta has over 240M opted-in end users and identities that large brands utilize to target in their campaigns," said KeyBanc analysts, led by Jackson Ader, in an investor note. "Because the core differentiator here is first-party data, industry worries [cookie deprecation, bulk email distribution changes, Apple's (AAPL) IDFA] have become direct tailwinds to Zeta's business."

KeyBanc forecasts scaled customers to continue growing in the mid-single digits through 2029. The bank also highlights that Zeta is investing in experienced sales reps to help it land major clients. It is also growing its channels in the connected TV and mobile segments.

KeyBanc set a price target of $40 on the stock.

Zeta shares have surged this year, as they have more than tripled since January from around $8 to closing at a record high of $33 on October 9.

"Our price target assumes a beta of 1.7x and a market risk premium of 6% to arrive at a 13.8% discount rate," Ader added. "Because we believe the Company will still be growing cash flow in the mid-teens in the out-years, we believe a 24x terminal multiple, a discount to the sector's current multiple, is warranted to get to our $40 price target."

data.title: Dam disaster agreement could curb Brazil lawsuits against Vale, BHP - Reuters
data.description:
An agreement by Vale (NYSE:VALE), BHP (BHP) and their Samarco joint venture to pay 170B reais (nearly $30B) in compensation for a deadly 2015 dam collapse in Brazil is expected to end more than 100 lawsuits against the companies and perhaps limit legal action abroad, Reuters reported Tuesday.

The agreement does not eliminate the possibility of new lawsuits related to damages if their connection with the dam collapse is proven at some point in the future, but the deal could end many civil actions against the miners in Brazil, and the companies expect that individual requests related to the Mariana disaster will be met in full by 2025, according to the report.

The mining companies reportedly also hope class action lawsuits filed in London and the Netherlands will dry up following the final deal.

The Pogust Goodhead law firm is leading one of the biggest court cases in U.K. legal history to determine whether BHP (BHP) is liable, and the case entered a decisive stage this week with the start of a 12-week trial.

"There is no possibility" the agreement in Brazil could invalidate the trial in England, a partner at Pogust Goodhead told Reuters.
